---
title: "Business Cycles & Policy Dynamics — Track Map"
---

Short-run fluctuations and the policy rules that shape them. The IS curve is
the entry point; the equilibrium-cycle chapters form a second, parallel branch.
Click a node to jump there.

```{mermaid}
graph LR
  IS[IS Curve] --> AD[Dynamic AD-AS + Taylor Rule]
  AD --> AX[AD-AS Exercises]
  LI[Lucas Island Model] --> EQ[Equilibrium Cycles w/ Persistence]
  EQ --> RBC[Real Business Cycles]
  click IS "../content/is_curve_model.html"
  click AD "../content/adassim.html"
  click AX "../content/adas_model.html"
  click LI "../content/lucas_island_model.html"
  click EQ "../content/equilibrium_model.html"
  click RBC "../content/rbc_simulation.html"
```

## Suggested path

1. [The IS Curve](../content/is_curve_model.qmd) — interest rates, demand, and goods-market equilibrium.
2. [Dynamic AD–AS with a Taylor Rule](../content/adassim.qmd) — inflation and output dynamics under a policy rule, including how the economy absorbs demand and supply shocks.
3. [AD–AS: Exercises & Case Studies](../content/adas_model.ipynb) — applied problems using the AD–AS framework.
4. [The Lucas Island Model](../content/lucas_island_model.qmd) — imperfect information as a source of real fluctuations.
5. [Equilibrium Business Cycles with Persistence](../content/equilibrium_model.qmd) — propagation that stretches shocks over time.
6. [The Real Business Cycle Model](../content/rbc_simulation.qmd) — fluctuations as the efficient response to productivity shocks.
